entities.name: SORGHUM
entities.description: The crop species studied for grain molding and phenolic content in its caryopses.

entities.name: PHENOLIC COMPOUNDS
entities.description: Chemical compounds quantified in sorghum caryopses, specifically free phenolic compounds.

entities.name: PHENOLIC ACIDS
entities.description: Chemical acids quantified in sorghum caryopses, including p-coumaric, ferulic, and caffeic acids.

entities.name: WET ENVIRONMENT
entities.description: Growth condition characterized by high moisture that promotes grain molding.

entities.name: DRY ENVIRONMENT
entities.description: Growth condition characterized by low moisture.

entities.name: GRAIN MOLDING
entities.description: The susceptibility or resistance of sorghum caryopses to mold growth.

entities.name: PERICARP COLOR
entities.description: The color of the sorghum pericarp, specifically white or red, which affects phenolic levels.

entities.name: PIGMENTED TESTA
entities.description: The presence or absence of pigmentation in the seed coat, associated with mold resistance.

entities.name: HUMID CONDITIONS
entities.description: Environmental conditions with high humidity that influence phenolic compound levels.

entities.name: ENDOSPERM TEXTURE
entities.description: The physical consistency of the sorghum endosperm, which varies with testa pigmentation.

entities.name: P-COUMARIC ACID
entities.description: A specific phenolic acid whose higher levels are related to mold susceptibility.

entities.name: FERULIC ACID
entities.description: A specific phenolic acid found in sorghum caryopses.

entities.name: CAFFEIC ACID
entities.description: A specific phenolic acid found in sorghum caryopses.

entities.name: QUANTIFICATION
entities.description: The process of measuring the levels of phenolic compounds and phenolic acids in caryopses.
